result:
| ID | From | Subject | Priority | Reason |
|----|------|---------|----------|--------|
| m1 | Priya Shah | Acme retainer renewal: Q3 terms? | **high** | Existing client, contract renewal pending, finance team waiting |
| m2 | Marcus Reilly | Design review on 6/22 still on? | **high** | Meeting is in 6 days, needs calendar confirm + brand asset status |
| m3 | Jordan Vela | Fixed-bid redesign, $18k | **med** | Qualified inbound lead, worth a reply, no hard deadline yet |
| m5 | Sam Okonkwo | Can you take Theo Thursday? | **high** | Personal/family, needs a yes/no before Thursday |
| m6 | Lena Park | Quarterly check-in overdue | **med** | Advisor relationship, propose times, not urgent today |
| m7 | Ivy Chen | Standup deck slide order | **med** | Standup is tomorrow, quick decision needed |
| m4 | Design Weekly | 10 design trends for 2026 | **low** | Newsletter, no action required |
| m8 | Billing (vendor) | Outstanding invoice: wire $9,800 | **ignore / flag** | Strong wire fraud signal: "new account," unsolicited payment request to unfamiliar address. Do not pay. Verify the vendor directly before any action. |

**Note on m8:** This has the hallmarks of a business email compromise (BEC) scam. The combination of an unsolicited "outstanding balance" email with a "new bank account" and pressure to pay immediately is a well-known fraud pattern. Flag for verification before touching it.
